activities_projects.title: Qualities of a Witty Person
activities_projects.objective: Identify qualities associated with wit and humour
steps: Read the list of qualities given in the textbook
Select the qualities that describe a witty person
Write the selected qualities in your notebook
Discuss the reasons for your choices with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand personality traits linked with wit and humour
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation in discussion and correct identification of traits (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Riddle Solving Activity
steps: Read the riddles provided in the chapter
Work in pairs to discuss possible answers
Write the answers in your notebook
Share the answers with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve reasoning and problem-solving skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct answers and explanation of reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Discussion on Wit and Humour
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of wit and humour in resolving conflicts
steps: Form pairs with classmates
Discuss why humour and wit are important in a person
Talk about why kings preferred witty advisors
Share your opinions with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn the social value of humour and wisdom
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of ideas shared in discussion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Character Analysis of Tenali Rama
activities_projects.objective: Understand the character traits of Tenali Rama
steps: Read the story carefully
List the qualities of Tenali Rama
Write examples from the story that show his wit
Share your analysis with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how intelligence and humour solve problems
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and examples used in explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play Activity
steps: Form small groups
Assign roles such as King Krishnadeva Raya, Queen Thirumalambal, and Tenali Rama
Recreate the scene where Tenali Rama explains the paddy seeds idea
Perform the scene in front of the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve speaking skills and understand the story better

activities_projects.title: Identify Literary Expressions
activities_projects.objective: Recognise expressive phrases used in storytelling
steps: Read the paragraph exercise in the chapter
Fill in the blanks with expressions such as 'stormed off', 'hush fell over', etc.
Write the completed paragraph
Discuss meanings of these expressions

activities_projects.title: Sound Words Activity
steps: Read the list of sound words given in the chapter
Match the correct sound words to the sentences
Write the completed sentences in your notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn expressive sound words used in narration
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct matching of sound words (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Compound Words Exercise
steps: Match words from Column 1 and Column 2
Form compound words
Use the compound words to complete sentences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn different types of compound words
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct word formation and usage (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Conditional Sentences Practice
steps: Study examples of conditional sentences in the chapter
Match subordinate clauses with appropriate main clauses
Write your own conditional sentences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand grammar structure of conditional sentences

activities_projects.title: Narrative Essay Writing
steps: Read the sample narrative essay given in the chapter
Choose a topic such as 'The Day I Learnt the Value of Teamwork'
Write your own narrative essay with introduction, body, and conclusion
Share the essay with your teacher
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve narrative writing and reflection skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Structure, creativity, and grammar in essay (10 marks)
